summary: Mexican muralist, one of 'Los Tres Grandes' with Rivera and Siqueiros. Most commonly identified by The Epic of American Civilization (Baker Library, Dartmouth). Also known for Prometheus, The Trench, and House of Tears.

works.name: The Epic of American Civilization (Baker Library, Dartmouth)
works.description: Sprawling mural at Dartmouth's Baker Library. Includes Departure of Quetzalcoatl and skeleton giving birth atop books.

clues.clue: Mural at Dartmouth's Baker Library; chronicles native peoples, Europeans, industry; skeleton giving birth on books
examples: painted on the walls of the library at Dartmouth College

clues.clue: Departure of Quetzalcoatl: god with golden beard, white robe, pointing from red pyramid with black serpents
examples: painted black serpents rising out of a tumultuous sea around a god with a white robe

clues.clue: Mexican muralist; contemporary of Rivera and Siqueiros
examples: this Mexican muralist, a contemporary of Diego Rivera

clues.clue: Prometheus: man on knees reaching for fire beyond the painting
examples: The title character is on his knees as he seems to reach beyond the top of the painting

clues.clue: The Trench: shirtless men with machine gun rounds smashed against rocks
examples: shirtless men carrying machine gun rounds are smashed against rocks

clues.clue: House of Tears: watercolors of prostitutes
examples: compiled watercolors of prostitutes into his House of Tears series

comprehensive_summary: José Clemente Orozco was a Mexican muralist alongside Rivera and Siqueiros. The Epic of American Civilization at Dartmouth's Baker Library chronicles native peoples, Europeans, and industry, including the Departure of Quetzalcoatl and a skeleton giving birth atop books. Prometheus shows a man reaching for fire. The Trench depicts shirtless men. House of Tears compiled watercolors of prostitutes.
